definition:
πλεονάζω
 (πλέον), [in LXX for עֲדַף, רָבָה, etc. ;] 
  I. Intrans. 
   1. Of persons; 
   (a) to abound in (Arist.); 
   (b) to superabound: 2Co.8:15 (LXX). 
   2. Of things, to abound, superabound: Rom.5:20 6:1, 2Co.4:15, Php.4:17, 2Th.1:3, 2Pe.1:8. 
   II. Trans. (Num.26:54, Psa.71:21; not cl, see Lft., Notes, 48 f.), to make to abound: 1Th.3:12 (cf. ὑπερ-πλεονάζω).†
 SYN.: περισσεύω, q.v  (AS)
